title: The One With Mac And C.H.E.E.S.E.
weakest_section: 19:30-20:45 range with extended serious relationship conversation

jokes.text: What does that tell you? / That refrigerators don't live as long as people?
jokes.setup: Joey implies Rachel's presence caused the fridge to break
jokes.punchline: Rachel's literal, logical response deflates his supernatural accusation
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with perfect character work - Joey's illogical blame vs Rachel's dry logic. Economical and well-earned.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The fridge broke because you were near it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, that doesn't make any sense. I didn't do anything to it.

jokes.text: Of course I'm joking. I don't take checks.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if Joey's joking about payment methods
jokes.punchline: Joey clarifies he's joking about taking checks, not about wanting money
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection - we expect him to back down, instead he doubles down with a practical concern.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're joking, right?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm joking about the checks. I definitely want the money.

jokes.text: Thank God, you're pretty.
jokes.setup: Rachel's confused about Joey's payment logic
jokes.punchline: Joey implies she's intellectually deficient but compensates with looks
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey line - perfectly in character but relies on 'pretty but dumb' trope. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, how does that even make sense?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It doesn't have to make sense, Rach. You're beautiful.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Thank you, Chandler.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests Joey as a cute guy for Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's polite but dismissive response to an obviously bad suggestion
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat - polite rejection of absurd suggestion. Functional but not special.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey's cute, right?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, um, sure. In a... Joey way.

jokes.text: Ralph mumbles a lot. / He mumbles when you're not attentive?
jokes.setup: Rachel complains about Ralph Lauren mumbling
jokes.punchline: Monica suggests it's Rachel's fault for not paying attention
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Monica's direct challenge to Rachel's work attitude. Sharp and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't believe Ralph Lauren mumbles so much. I was at this meeting with him and I couldn't understand a word he said.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you actually listened instead of just thinking about yourself for five seconds, you'd hear what he's saying.

jokes.text: So he has to be a male who has at least $50. / So close.
jokes.setup: Monica lists minimum requirements for a date
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies he almost meets these basic standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler self-deprecation - economical and character-specific. The timing of 'So close' is key.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, here are my minimum requirements for a date. He has to be smart, he has to be funny, he has to be kind...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm two out of three.

jokes.text: Isn't she cute? Nope.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if Elizabeth is cute
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt, immediate denial
jokes.explanation: Chandler's trademark bluntness. Maximum economy - one word destroys Ross's pride. Classic timing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Is Elizabeth cute?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Why is she leaving? Is it a school night and she has homework?
jokes.setup: Elizabeth leaves quickly
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats her like a child with a curfew
jokes.explanation: Good age-gap humor - Chandler infantilizing Ross's young girlfriend. Well-constructed setup.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Where are you going? It's only 9 o'clock!

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: I have to go home.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: On a school night? What about your homework?

jokes.text: It's just been so long since I've been to Chuck E. Cheese.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Elizabeth's birthday
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests she'd have her party at a children's restaurant
jokes.explanation: Escalates the age joke with specific, absurd reference. Chuck E. Cheese is perfect specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you know, with Elizabeth being so young, maybe she'd have her party at a children's restaurant. Like Chuck E. Cheese's or something.

jokes.text: A box of Thin Mints and some Tagalongs.
jokes.setup: Chandler's Girl Scout cookie reference
jokes.punchline: He knows specific cookie names, treating Elizabeth like a Girl Scout
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific cookie knowledge. Shows Chandler's commitment to the bit.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? You're like a Girl Scout cookie.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, you're like a Thin Mint - you're very popular, but you only come around once a year.

jokes.text: What is she, like, 12?
jokes.setup: Ross defends his relationship
jokes.punchline: Chandler escalates age jokes to absurd extreme
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation but economical delivery. Sets up the running age gag.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She's not that much younger than me.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Right, and King Kong wasn't that much bigger than a regular ape. I mean, sure, he's a little older, but when you break it down, they're basically the same size. She's probably been alive for like, what, six years? That's practically a full life for some insects.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not saying you're robbing the cradle, but you might want to check if she needs a nap before dinner.

jokes.text: Yeah. Do we have any Fruit Roll-Ups?
jokes.setup: Monica asks about their relationship communication
jokes.punchline: Chandler responds with childish snack request
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deflection - avoids serious conversation with absurd specificity. Character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So how are things with you and Janice?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, we're communicating now. She said we need to talk about our feelings more often.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's good, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, so I told her my feelings. I said, 'I have a feeling I'd like some Cheetos right now.'

jokes.text: A face ass? / A chin dimple.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions her guy has 'one of these' (pointing to chin)
jokes.punchline: Monica misinterprets the gesture hilariously
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy misunderstanding. 'Face ass' is memorably absurd and perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: My guy has one of these.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A chin?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, a job.

jokes.text: No. Our guy's just a floating head.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if they've seen their guy's body
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan response to obvious question
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback. The absurd image of a floating head works well.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I'm dating him, so... yes.

jokes.text: Like there are police for that.
jokes.setup: Monica says masseurs aren't supposed to look
jokes.punchline: Phoebe dismisses professional ethics with absurd enforcement image
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - dismissing rules through absurd literalism. Character-specific and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Masseurs aren't supposed to look.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, come on! What are they gonna do, fire me? I'm a self-employed massage therapist!

jokes.text: You look? You massaged me. / I know.
jokes.setup: Chandler realizes Phoebe looked at his body
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual confirmation of his fear
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Chandler's horror met with Phoebe's nonchalant admission. Economy of 'I know' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, you looked at my body?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: Chandler funny?
jokes.setup: Claim that their guy is funny
jokes.punchline: Monica questions this using Chandler as reference point
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Monica using Chandler as standard for unfunny. Economical insult.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's funny? Have you met Chandler?

jokes.text: Do you want our guy to be your guy?
jokes.setup: Escalating competition over whose guy is better
jokes.punchline: Monica suggests switching sides mid-competition
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation - the competitive spiral reaches logical breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. If your guy is so great, why don't we just switch? You take mine, I'll take yours.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? No! That's not how this works!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Come on, live a little! See what the other side has to offer.

jokes.text: Cold cuts, ice cream, limes. What was in that brown jar? / That's still in there? / Not anymore.
jokes.setup: Joey ate everything when fridge broke
jokes.punchline: He ate something so old/gross Rachel thought it was still there
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character moment - Joey's willingness to eat anything disgusting. Three-beat structure works.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, what did you eat?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't know, it was in the back of the fridge. It had like three different colors of mold on it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God! You ate that?!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was hungry!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, I thought that was still in there. I was saving it... for science.

jokes.text: Look, okay, suppose we were a divorced couple. And I got custody of the kid. Right? Now suppose the kid dies and I gotta buy a new kid.
jokes.setup: Joey tries to explain why Rachel owes him money
jokes.punchline: Absurd analogy comparing fridge to dead child
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Joey logic. The casual way he compares appliances to dead children is memorably dark and specific to his character.

jokes.text: Give me $400! / Thank God, you're pretty.
jokes.setup: Joey's frustrated demand after absurd analogy fails
jokes.punchline: Returns to his earlier assessment of Rachel's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Effective callback - same line but funnier in context of his failed complex analogy. Shows consistency of character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: See? This is why I said you were stupid!

jokes.text: Oh, a student I don't know.
jokes.setup: Student approaches Ross outside his office
jokes.punchline: Ross's deliberately obvious observation to avoid appearance of familiarity
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Ross overcorrecting to hide his relationship. The formality is perfectly awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Hey Professor Geller, got a minute?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, hello. Yes, I have a minute. You are a student. At this university. Whom I teach. In a class. That meets on certain days of the week.

jokes.text: Making out in your office. / Of course. Why don't we go inside.
jokes.setup: Elizabeth mentions making out in Ross's office in public
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately ushers her inside, defeating the point
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Ross's panic makes him act suspiciously. The immediate reversal works.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: We could make out in your office. Right here. In public.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Get in here!

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: That kind of defeats the purpose.

jokes.text: So I will take one box of the Thin Mints.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to get rid of professor colleague
jokes.punchline: Uses Chandler's Girl Scout cookie reference to treat Elizabeth as child
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier Girl Scout joke. Shows how the age humor has infected Ross's thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Elizabeth, I've been thinking... you know how Girl Scouts sell cookies?

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: What? No, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, they're always trying to grow up too fast. And I think that's what's happening with us. You're like a Girl Scout cookie - delicious now, but illegal if I enjoy you.

jokes.text: Then you distract her with a Barbie doll.
jokes.setup: Ross worried about Elizabeth getting upset
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests treating her like a child who can be distracted with toys
jokes.explanation: Continues age theme with specific toy reference. Barbie is perfect choice for insulting specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't know what to do. Elizabeth is going to be so upset.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Just treat her like a child. You know, distract her with toys or something shiny.

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? / What the hell am I doing? You just broke my fridge!
jokes.setup: Rachel confronts Joey about pushing delivery man
jokes.punchline: Joey deflects by blaming her for fridge breaking during push
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Joey's circular logic and immediate blame deflection. Character-appropriate response.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, why did you push the delivery man?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I didn't push him, he fell into me. Besides, if your fridge wasn't so close to the door, none of this would have happened.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: My fridge? You're the one who pushed him into it!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you're the one who bought a fridge made of tissue paper.

jokes.text: You think I don't know what breaks my fridge? / Excuse me! / Well, what do you know? Broken!
jokes.setup: Joey claims to know what breaks fridges
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves his point by breaking another fridge
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy setup - Joey's expertise immediately disproven. Perfect timing and irony.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know exactly what breaks fridges.

dialogue.speaker: Delivery man
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: This.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: ...I broke another fridge.

jokes.text: Doorknob? / Yeah. It kind of grows on you.
jokes.setup: Elizabeth questions Ross's earlier doorknob comment
jokes.punchline: Ross tries to make it seem like an ongoing thing rather than panic
jokes.explanation: Decent callback - Ross trying to normalize his panic response. Shows his awkward attempts at recovery.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: What was that thing you said about the doorknob?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, that? Yeah, I've been thinking about doorknobs a lot lately. It's kind of a thing I'm into now.

jokes.text: No. / I think you misunderstood what I was saying.
jokes.setup: Ross assumes Elizabeth wants him to come on trip
jokes.punchline: Elizabeth reveals she was just informing him, not inviting
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment - Ross's assumption backfires spectacularly. The 'No' is perfectly brutal and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So what time should I pick you up tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Pick me up? I was just telling you I was going away.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Right, and I'm coming with you.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: No, you're not.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Because I didn't invite you!

jokes.text: Spring vacation, you're doing nice things with your grandparents. Spring break, you're doing frat guys.
jokes.setup: Monica asks difference between spring vacation and spring break
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt distinction between wholesome and sexual activities
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor with perfect contrast structure. 'Doing frat guys' is memorably blunt and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What's the difference between spring vacation and spring break?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, spring vacation is when you go somewhere with your family and actually *do* things. Spring break is when you go somewhere and do... other things.

jokes.text: I can see it now. 'Look, Dad, it's the Sphinx, wahoo!'
jokes.setup: Monica mentions going to Egypt with her dad
jokes.punchline: Chandler imagines her treating historic monument like spring break party
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd image - mixing ancient Egypt with party atmosphere. The 'wahoo' callback ties it together perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Egypt? Monica, you're going to Egypt? Oh, I can see it now. You at the pyramids, 'WOOO! Spring break!' You climbing on the Sphinx, 'Chandler, take a picture of me!'

jokes.text: What? I read that.
jokes.setup: Sebastian mentions Field and Stream magazine
jokes.punchline: Rachel defensively claims to read outdoorsy magazine
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Rachel's insecurity about appearing sophisticated. Quick defensive response feels natural.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I read Field and Stream!

jokes.text: Yeah, and I found you one too, who is not a weirdo. / You know what though, guys, I really appreciate that... but I'm gonna take Sebastian.
jokes.setup: Phoebe emphasizes her guy is not a weirdo
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately chooses Sebastian instead, making the emphasis pointless
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke - Phoebe's defensive qualification immediately rendered moot. The quick dismissal works well.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: He's not a weirdo!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'll take Sebastian.

jokes.text: Are you funny? Tell us a joke! / I just wanted to have coffee with Rachel. / Well, so do a lot of people.
jokes.setup: Friends aggressively vet Sebastian with demands
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dismissive response to his reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of protective friend behavior. Phoebe's dismissive 'so do a lot of people' is perfectly cutting.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Who are you and what do you want with our friend?

dialogue.speaker: Sebastian
dialogue.line: I'd just like to take her to dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's not gonna work for us.

jokes.text: Turns out he is kind of funny.
jokes.setup: Sebastian leaves after their aggressive questioning
jokes.punchline: Monica finds his departure amusing, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Good ironic beat - Monica completely misreading the situation. Her satisfaction is perfectly misplaced.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I guess we were a little hard on him.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But hey, at least he left! That's what we wanted, right?

jokes.text: Yeah, but 'Sebastian'? What is that, a cat's name?
jokes.setup: Rachel defends Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Monica dismisses him based purely on his name
jokes.explanation: Decent name-based humor. Monica's judgmental nature coming through in petty criticism.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's actually really nice once you get to know him.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: His name is Sebastian. There's nothing to get to know.

jokes.text: 'Hey, Sebastian, would you like to dance?' 'Okay. I gotta go.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Sebastian being easily scared
jokes.punchline: Chandler acts out Sebastian fleeing at simple invitation
jokes.explanation: Good character impression - Chandler's theatrical demonstration of Sebastian's timidity. Well-structured mini-scene.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, Sebastian's easily scared. So when someone says 'Hey, want to come over?' he's like *jumps back in terror* 'AHHH! A SOCIAL INVITATION! I'M OUTTA HERE!'

jokes.text: There you are! You broke my fridge. You owe me $400. / Okay, sure. / Really? / Technically, you owe me $600 for sending out happy thoughts on your last 10 auditions.
jokes.setup: Joey demands money from Phoebe, she agrees
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reveals she's billing him for psychic services
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and perfect Phoebe logic. The specific 'happy thoughts' billing is brilliantly absurd and character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You owe me money.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Fine, I'll pay you back.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: But that'll be $200 for my psychic services telling you that you were going to ask.

jokes.text: In 1981, you were 13. / So? I drove down, sold T-shirts. Had a blast. You know who knows how to party? Drunk college chicks.
jokes.setup: Ross points out Joey was 13 during spring break '81
jokes.punchline: Joey casually admits to inappropriate behavior as a minor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Joey's casual admission of questionable teenage entrepreneurship. Character-appropriate boundary-crossing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, you were 13 during spring break in '81.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, so?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So? Joey, that means you were a minor!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, I was there.

jokes.text: If she's got enough money for that, she can certainly pay for the fridge she broke.
jokes.setup: Ross describes Elizabeth's expensive spring break hotel
jokes.punchline: Joey connects it to his fridge payment obsession
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Joey's single-track mind connecting everything to his fridge payment. Perfect Joey logic.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A mini-bar in every room? Dude, that's like having a fridge that costs money!

jokes.text: Ross, are you okay? / What? / Are you into crafts at all?
jokes.setup: Elizabeth asks if Ross is okay after his strange behavior
jokes.punchline: Ross deflects with random craft question instead of addressing issue
jokes.explanation: Good cringe - Ross's panic leading to completely random deflection. The specificity of 'crafts' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Are you okay? You've been acting really strange.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? I'm fine. Hey, do you know how to make a birdhouse out of popsicle sticks?

jokes.text: It's a bathing suit? / To wear in front of people?
jokes.setup: Elizabeth shows Ross her bathing suit
jokes.punchline: Ross questions the basic purpose of swimwear
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment - Ross's jealousy making him question basic clothing concepts. The incredulity is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. What is that?

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: It's a bathing suit.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no. A bathing suit is supposed to cover... stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: It does cover stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Elizabeth, I can see your... everything!

jokes.text: But why put off something till tomorrow that you could do it right now? / Eldad, come here!
jokes.setup: Monica suggests not delaying the date meeting
jokes.punchline: Immediately ambushes Rachel with surprise introduction
jokes.explanation: Good setup for ambush comedy. Monica's controlling nature perfectly displayed through surprise tactics.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You shouldn't delay meeting him.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: In fact, I have a surprise for you!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, what did you do?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's here! Come on in!

jokes.text: A little blindsided but good.
jokes.setup: Eldad asks how Rachel is after surprise introduction
jokes.punchline: Rachel's honest assessment of being ambushed
jokes.explanation: Great understatement - Rachel's polite but honest reaction to ambush. 'Blindsided but good' is perfectly balanced.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I'm great! I love being ambushed by my boyfriend's secret family members. Really keeps things exciting!

jokes.text: This suit does not do justice to what's underneath it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe introduces Patrick to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes inappropriate comment about his body
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate inappropriate comment. Phoebe's lack of social filters on full display.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Rachel, this is Patrick. Patrick, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wow, those are some really impressive pecs. Do you work out, or is that just from carrying around that giant thing in your pants?

jokes.text: Sit down, we're winning.
jokes.setup: Eldad tries to leave the awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Monica treats dating like a competition to be won
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness - treating human connection as a game to win. Economical and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, where are you going? We're not done here. I have a whole strategy for how we're going to win this.

dialogue.speaker: Eldad
dialogue.line: Win what? It's a date, not a championship.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Exactly! And I'm going to help you dominate. First, we need to assess the competition, identify weaknesses, and execute a flawless game plan.

jokes.text: This isn't really worth the free massage.
jokes.setup: Escalating awkwardness of the forced date setup
jokes.punchline: Patrick reveals he was bribed and it's not worth it
jokes.explanation: Good revelation humor - exposes the friends' manipulation while showing the situation's absurdity through outside perspective.

jokes.text: No offense to you guys. Really, congratulations on... all the cash.
jokes.setup: Rachel addressing Eldad and Patrick after telling off her friends
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically congratulates them on whatever they're being paid
jokes.explanation: Good polite sarcasm - Rachel staying classy while making her point about the obvious bribery. Well-delivered burn.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wow, congratulations. Whatever they're paying you, it's clearly not enough.

jokes.text: It was pretty funny when I hid it for a while.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about Elizabeth's bathing suit
jokes.punchline: Ross admits to hiding her swimsuit
jokes.explanation: Good cringe confession - Ross admitting to controlling behavior while trying to play it off as cute. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I hid your bathing suit.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: You what?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I hid your bathing suit so you wouldn't go to the beach without me.

jokes.text: Sometimes you get 30, sometimes it's 4 and... more often than not, it's just milk.
jokes.setup: Ross warning about unreliable sunblock SPF
jokes.punchline: Escalates to completely absurd claim about milk substitution
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to absurdity - Ross's jealousy driving him to make up increasingly ridiculous claims. The milk detail is perfectly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, the key to sun protection is SPF. But here's the thing - most people don't realize that SPF numbers aren't always accurate. I once used a sunblock that said SPF 50, but I think it was actually SPF 3.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How could you tell?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I got burned so badly, the doctor said I looked like a rotisserie chicken. And that's not even the worst part - I discovered that if you don't have sunblock, you can just rub milk on yourself instead.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Milk?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes! Milk! In fact, I read that if you use expired milk, it's actually SPF 1000. Some people have replaced their entire skincare routine with a simple buttermilk bath.

jokes.text: I wanna say a disease.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what the charity was for
jokes.punchline: Rachel's uncertain response about disease vs trees
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier uncertainty. The tentative 'I wanna say' perfectly captures her continued cluelessness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What was the charity for?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Um, I think it was for disease... or trees? I don't know, there was a silent auction and I just kept bidding.

jokes.text: Professor Geller.
jokes.setup: Elizabeth appears on MTV spring break coverage
jokes.punchline: She addresses Ross formally while clearly drunk/partying
jokes.explanation: Great visual payoff - Elizabeth's formal address while clearly in party mode. Perfect vindication of everyone's spring break warnings.

dialogue.speaker: Elizabeth
dialogue.line: Oh my God, hi Dr. Geller!

jokes.text: To be 13 again...
jokes.setup: Elizabeth's spring break debauchery on TV
jokes.punchline: Joey's wistful comment referencing his earlier inappropriate teen experiences
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's earlier inappropriate teen confession. The wistful delivery makes it funnier and more disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Those were the days...

tmdb_overview: Joey gets an audition for the lead character in a new TV show. When the audition is rescheduled, Chandler takes the message but forgets to tell Joey. When Joey misses the audition, he gets upset and the group reminisces about their mishaps in the past.
insight: Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E. leans hard on character work—53 jokes in 44 minutes, mostly deadpan.
